metadata.dispatch_line: Possible stalking in progress at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive, report of unwanted messages and gifts from an ex-partner

scenario.dispatch_line: Possible stalking in progress at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive, report of unwanted messages and gifts from an ex-partner

facts.stalking_01: Call recorded at 14:45, incident occurred at 13:30, dispatch at 14:40, and arrival at 14:50
facts.stalking_02: Victim, Evelyn Patel, and suspect, Julian Sanchez, were in a dating relationship for 2 years prior to the incident
facts.stalking_03: Relationship was marked by frequent arguments, with 3 incidents of verbal intimidation and 1 incident of physical violence in the past 6 months
facts.stalking_04: Stalking behaviors included repeated phone calls, sending unwanted flowers, and showing up at Evelyn's workplace
facts.stalking_05: No valid protection order in place
facts.stalking_06: Witnesses, Rachel Lee and Michael Kim, were interviewed and documented, stating they saw Julian lurking around Evelyn's apartment complex
facts.stalking_07: Previous incidents of stalking behavior were documented, including an incident 2 weeks prior where Julian showed up at Evelyn's favorite coffee shop
facts.stalking_08: Evidence collected includes a bouquet of flowers with a threatening note, and a security camera footage of Julian's car parked outside Evelyn's apartment
facts.stalking_09: Electronic evidence collected includes a recording of a threatening phone call from Julian to Evelyn
facts.stalking_10: All incidents of stalking behavior on Evelyn's cell phone were captured, including 20 missed calls and 5 threatening text messages
facts.stalking_11: Scene was described as a residential apartment complex, with a security camera and witness statements
facts.stalking_12: No weapons or objects used in the incident
facts.stalking_13: Evelyn stated she was feeling 'scared' and 'anxious' during the incident, and was thinking 'I need to get away from him'
facts.stalking_14: Evidence of fear articulated in the report includes Evelyn's statement that she was 'afraid for her life'
facts.stalking_15: Threats clearly documented, including a statement from Julian saying 'I'll never let you go'
facts.stalking_16: No use of coercion or force articulated in the report
facts.stalking_17: No visible or non-visible injuries documented
facts.stalking_18: No property damage, theft, or burglary documented
facts.stalking_19: Photos taken of the bouquet of flowers and the security camera footage, with details recorded
facts.stalking_20: No report of being strangled or choked
facts.stalking_21: Evelyn did not require medical attention
facts.stalking_22: Spontaneous statements captured, including Evelyn's statement that 'I'm afraid of him'
facts.stalking_23: Elements of the stalking crime thoroughly articulated, including repeated unwanted contact and threats
facts.stalking_24: Victim's safety planning documented, including a plan to stay with a friend and change her phone number
facts.stalking_25: Information about obtaining an order of protection provided to Evelyn
facts.stalking_26: Information about local service providers, including a domestic violence hotline and a counseling service, supplied to Evelyn
facts.stalking_27: Threat/risk assessment tool utilized with Evelyn, indicating a high risk of further stalking behavior
facts.stalking_28: Use of a stalking kit and/or stalking log discussed with Evelyn
facts.stalking_29: No information about Julian's access to weapons
facts.stalking_30: No information about Julian's emotional state
facts.stalking_31: No history of protection orders against Julian
facts.stalking_32: No prohibition from possessing firearms due to a conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence or a qualifying order of protection
facts.stalking_33: No active warrants out for Julian

interview_transcript.content: Hi, my name is Officer [name]. I'm here to help you today. I want you to know that you're safe speaking with me. Some of my questions may be detailed, but I ask them because I want to understand exactly what happened and make sure you're protected. Everything you tell me is important. To start — can you tell me, in your own words, what's been happening and what made you call today?

(nervously) Okay... I've been getting these calls and flowers from my ex, Julian. He's been showing up at my work and outside my apartment. It's been really scary. Today was the last straw, I guess. He sent me a bouquet with a threatening note, and I just felt like I had to do something. I was afraid for my life, so I called for help. (pausing, taking a deep breath) That's when I called you, at 14:45. The incident happened a bit earlier, around 13:30.

Thank you for sharing that with me, Evelyn. I can hear that this has been very frightening. You mentioned a bouquet with a threatening note — I'd like to understand exactly what it said. Can you tell me the exact words that were written in that note, as best you can remember them?

(hesitantly) It said... "I'll never let you go." Those were the exact words. It was handwritten, and it looked like he had written it in a hurry. The handwriting was messy, but the words were clear. (pausing, looking nervous) Just seeing those words made me feel really scared and trapped.

That note sounds very alarming. I want to make sure I capture everything accurately. Where were you when you received that bouquet today — was it at your apartment at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive, or somewhere else? And who delivered it or how did it arrive?

(shaking her head) It was delivered to my apartment, yes, 1457 Cedarbrook Drive. I didn't see who delivered it, but I found it outside my door when I came home from work. I didn't order any flowers, and I knew right away that it was from Julian. (nervously) I didn't touch the note at first, I just saw the flowers and the card, and I knew I had to call for help.
Thank you. Can you tell me Julian's full name, and can you describe your relationship with him — how long were you together, when did you break up, and how did things end between you?

(taking a deep breath) His full name is Julian Sanchez. We were together for about 2 years. I don't know, it was a pretty tumultuous relationship, I guess. We argued a lot, and there were some incidents... (pausing, choosing her words carefully) He was verbally intimidating sometimes, and there was one time he got physical with me. We broke up, but he wouldn't leave me alone. He just kept calling and showing up, like I said. (nervously) It's been really hard to get him to stop.

I appreciate you sharing that. When did you break up with Julian, and when did the unwanted calls and showing up start? I also want to make sure I understand — you mentioned he got physical with you once. Can you tell me what happened during that incident, and approximately when that was?

(hesitantly) We broke up... I don't know, it's been a few months, I think. The calls and showing up started pretty much right after. (pausing, collecting her thoughts) The physical incident... it was about 6 months ago, I think. We were arguing, and he got angry. He pushed me, and I fell. It was really scary. (nervously) After that, I tried to stay away from him, but he just wouldn't leave me alone. He kept calling and showing up, saying he was sorry and that he loved me, but it didn't feel like love. It felt like... control, or something. (shaking her head) I don't know, it's all really confusing.

You're doing really well. I want to make sure I understand the full pattern of what's been happening since you broke up. Can you walk me through what kinds of contact Julian has been making — how often has he called or texted, how often has he shown up, and where has he shown up? For example, you mentioned your workplace and your apartment — how many times has he come to each of those places?

(nervously) Okay... He's been calling me a lot, like, every day, sometimes multiple times a day. And texting, too - I've gotten so many texts from him. (pausing, thinking) I'd say he's shown up at my workplace at least 5 or 6 times, and at my apartment... (shaking her head) I don't know, it feels like every other day, sometimes. He's also shown up at my favorite coffee shop, which is really scary because it feels like he's following me everywhere. (hesitantly) And then there are the flowers and gifts - he keeps sending them to me, even though I've told him to stop. It's just really overwhelming and scary. (taking a deep breath) I've been trying to avoid him, but it feels like he's always finding me.

That sounds very frightening, and you are right to be concerned. You mentioned he's sent many texts and messages — what has he been saying in them? Have any of the messages contained threats, demands, or anything that frightened you? And have you saved any of those texts, voicemails, or messages — do you still have them on your phone?

(scared) Yeah, some of the messages have been really scary. He's said things like "I'll never let you go" and "You'll never be able to get away from me". (nervously) He's also sent me messages saying that he's watching me, and that he knows where I am. It's really creepy. (pausing, thinking) I've saved some of the texts and messages on my phone, yeah. I have a record of all the calls and texts he's sent me, including the threatening ones. (hesitantly) I've also recorded one of his voicemails, where he's saying some really scary things. It's all still on my phone.

I'm glad you saved all of that — that's very important evidence. You mentioned he said in messages that he's watching you and knows where you are. Do you have any reason to believe he might be tracking your location — for example, through your phone, a GPS device, or any shared accounts or apps? Have you noticed anything suspicious on your devices or car?

(concerned) I don't think he's tracking my location through my phone or anything, but... (pausing, thinking) I have noticed that he seems to know where I am a lot of the time. Like, I'll be at a store or at a friend's house, and he'll send me a message saying "I know you're there" or something like that. (nervously) It's really scary, because it feels like he's always watching me. As for my car, I haven't noticed anything suspicious, but... (hesitantly) I did notice that one of my friends, Rachel, saw him lurking around my apartment complex a few days ago. And another friend, Michael, said he saw him too. They both told me about it, and it really freaked me out.

Your friends Rachel and Michael are important witnesses. Do you have their last names and contact information so we can follow up with them? Also, do you know approximately when they saw Julian lurking near your apartment complex?

(nodding) Yeah, Rachel's last name is Lee, and Michael's is Kim. I can give you their phone numbers if you need them. (pausing, thinking) I think they both saw him a few days ago... maybe two or three days ago? I'm not really sure, but they told me about it recently. (nervously) They said they saw him just kind of hanging around the complex, looking around. It was really suspicious, and it scared them too. (hesitantly) I can try to find their numbers for you, if you need them.

Yes, please do get those numbers if you can — we will note that as a follow-up item. I also want to ask: how has all of this affected your daily life? Has it changed what you do, where you go, or how you feel day to day? What are you most afraid might happen?

(sighing) It's been really hard. I've been trying to avoid going out as much, because I'm always looking over my shoulder, wondering if he's going to show up. I've changed my routine, trying to be more unpredictable, but it's exhausting. (nervously) I've also been having trouble sleeping, because I'm always worried that he's going to show up at my apartment. I've been feeling really anxious and scared all the time. (pausing, looking down) I'm most afraid that he's going to hurt me, or that he's going to show up somewhere and I won't be able to get away. (whispering) I'm just really scared of him.

I hear you, and your safety is our priority. Is there currently any type of protection order or restraining order in place against Julian? Have you ever sought one before, or has he had any prior protection orders against him from anyone else that you know of?

(shaking her head) No, there's no protection order in place right now. I've thought about getting one, but I haven't done it yet. (nervously) I don't know if he's had any protection orders against him before, but I don't think so. At least, I've never heard of anything like that. (pausing, thinking) I've been trying to deal with this on my own, but it's just gotten too much. I feel like I need help now.
Do you know whether Julian owns any firearms or has access to any weapons? Have you ever seen him with a gun, or has he ever mentioned owning one?

(shaking her head) I don't think he owns any guns, and I've never seen him with one. We never really talked about guns or anything like that. (nervously) I don't know if he has access to any weapons, but I've never seen him with anything like that. He's just been using words and showing up places to scare me, I think. (pausing, thinking) I don't know, maybe I'm wrong, but I've never thought of him as someone who would use a gun or anything like that.
Do you know whether Julian has any prior arrests or active warrants? Has he had any run-ins with police before, either involving you or anyone else that you're aware of?

(shaking her head) I don't think so. I've never heard of him being arrested or having any warrants out for him. (nervously) We've had some arguments and stuff, but I don't think he's ever had any trouble with the police before. At least, he's never told me about anything like that. (pausing, thinking) I don't know, maybe I'm wrong, but I've never thought of him as someone who would have a record or anything like that.
Are you physically safe right now? Do you have any injuries — either from today or from the time he pushed you? Do you need any medical attention?

(shaking her head) I'm physically safe right now, I think. I don't have any injuries from today. (pausing, thinking) And from the time he pushed me, I didn't really get hurt too badly. I mean, I fell and got a little bruised, but it wasn't too serious. (nervously) I don't need any medical attention, no. I'm just really shaken up and scared, I guess. (sighing) I just want to feel safe again.

Is the bouquet and the note still at your apartment right now? I'd like to have it photographed and preserved as evidence. Also, has Julian tried to contact you through social media, email, or through other people besides directly calling and texting you?

(nodding) Yeah, the bouquet and the note are still at my apartment. I didn't touch them after I found them, so they should still be there. (nervously) You can send someone to get them, if you need to. I don't want to go back there right now, though. (pausing, thinking) As for other ways of contacting me, he's tried to reach me through social media a few times. He's sent me messages on Facebook and Instagram, but I've blocked him on both of those. (hesitantly) He's also tried to contact me through mutual friends, but I've told them not to talk to him about me. (sighing) He's just really relentless, and it's hard to get away from him.

Before you blocked Julian on Facebook and Instagram, did you screenshot any of those messages? And where are you right now — are you at your apartment or somewhere else? Do you have a safe place to stay tonight if you need one?

(nodding) Yeah, I did screenshot some of the messages before I blocked him. They're still on my phone, I think. (pausing, checking her phone) Yeah, I have them here. (nervously) I can show them to you if you need to see them. (looking up) And I'm not at my apartment right now. I'm... (pausing, thinking) I'm at the police station, I guess. I came here after I called for help. (hesitantly) As for a safe place to stay tonight, I was thinking of going to a friend's house. She's offered to let me stay with her for a while, until this all gets sorted out. (nervously) I don't feel safe going back to my apartment right now.

I want to make sure we've covered everything. Can you tell me where you work so we can document that location as well? Also — has Julian ever shown up or contacted you anywhere else, like at a gym, a family member's home, or any other regular place you go?

(nervously) I work at a office building downtown. I'm an administrative assistant. (pausing, thinking) As for other places, he's shown up at my favorite coffee shop a few times. And he's also shown up at my sister's house once, which was really scary for her. (shaking her head) He's also tried to contact me through my family members, which is really frustrating. (sighing) I feel like I've had to change my whole routine and avoid all the places I used to go, just to try to stay away from him. (nervously) It's been really hard.

Thank you, Evelyn. I want to let you know that I will be providing you with information about protection orders, local victim services, and a stalking log — which is a tool to help you document incidents going forward. Before I do that, is there anything else you want me to know? Is there anything Julian has said to you directly — in person or by phone — that you haven't told me yet, or anything you said to him? And has he ever said anything about what he would do if you didn't come back to him?

(hesitantly) There is one thing... (pausing, thinking) He's said a few times that if I don't come back to him, he'll make sure I regret it. He's said that he'll never let me go, and that I'll always be his. (nervously) It's really scary, because I don't know what he's capable of. (pausing, collecting her thoughts) As for what I've said to him, I've just told him to leave me alone, to stop contacting me. I've tried to be firm, but it's hard when he's being so aggressive and scary. (shaking her head) I don't know, I just feel like I'm walking on eggshells all the time, waiting for him to do something else. (sighing) I just want it to stop.

Those statements are very important and I want to make sure they are documented. Do you have a current address or phone number for Julian Sanchez? Also, do you know what he looks like — approximate age, height, build, or any other identifying details that would help us locate him?

(nervously) Yeah, I have his phone number, but I don't know if it's still the same. (pausing, thinking) It's... (providing the phone number). As for his address, I'm not really sure. We used to live together, but I don't know where he's living now. (thinking) He's... (describing his physical appearance) about 5'10", medium build, brown hair, brown eyes. He has a tattoo of a snake on his left arm, and a scar above his right eyebrow. (pausing, thinking) He's 32 years old, I think. (nervously) I don't know, I'm trying to remember everything, but it's all really scary.

Thank you. Is the phone number you have for Julian the same number the calls and texts have been coming from? And just to confirm — the evidence you have on your phone right now includes: saved text messages from Julian, a record of calls, a recorded voicemail, and screenshots of his Facebook and Instagram messages. Is that correct? Is there anything else on your phone or anywhere else that would be relevant?

(nodding) Yes, the phone number I have for Julian is the same number that the calls and texts have been coming from. (pausing, thinking) And yes, that's correct. The evidence I have on my phone includes saved text messages, a record of calls, a recorded voicemail, and screenshots of his Facebook and Instagram messages. (thinking) I also have a log of the dates and times he's shown up at my apartment and workplace, and the dates and times he's sent me flowers and gifts. (nervously) I've been trying to keep track of everything, so I can remember what's been happening. (pausing, thinking) I don't think there's anything else on my phone or anywhere else that would be relevant, but... (hesitantly) I do have a security camera at my apartment, and it may have caught footage of him showing up there. Would that be helpful?

Yes, absolutely — security camera footage is very important evidence. Is this your own personal camera, or is it the building's security system? Do you know how long the footage is stored? We will want to preserve that footage as soon as possible before it's overwritten.

(nodding) It's the building's security system. I'm not really sure how it works, but I know it's supposed to record footage of the lobby and the parking lot. (thinking) I think the footage is stored for a few weeks, but I'm not really sure. (nervously) I can ask my landlord or the building manager to find out for sure. (pausing, thinking) I remember that the camera is motion-activated, so it might have caught footage of Julian showing up at my apartment. (hopefully) Do you think that could be used as evidence?

Yes, that footage could be very valuable evidence. We will follow up with your building manager to preserve it. Evelyn, you've been very thorough and I appreciate your cooperation. I want to close by letting you know: I will document everything you've told me today in a police report. I'm providing you with information about protection orders, local victim advocacy services, and a stalking log to help you document any future incidents. Is there anything else — anything at all — that you feel is important for me to know before we conclude?

(shaking her head) I don't think so. I've told you everything I can think of. (pausing, looking relieved) Thank you for listening to me and for taking this seriously. It means a lot to me. (nervously) I just want to feel safe again, and I want Julian to leave me alone. (sighing) I'm just really scared, but I feel a little better now that I've talked to you. (looking up) What happens next? What can I expect?

narrative:
NARRATIVE REPORT — STALKING

DISPATCH / ARRIVAL

On the date of this report, at approximately 1445 hours, I responded to a call for service regarding a possible stalking in progress at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive. Dispatch reported unwanted messages and gifts from an ex-partner. The caller, Evelyn Patel, reported that the incident occurred at approximately 1330 hours, when she arrived home and discovered a bouquet of flowers with a threatening handwritten note outside her apartment door at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive. Ms. Patel called for help at 1445 hours. I interviewed Ms. Patel at the police station, where she had come after placing her call.

PERSONS INVOLVED

Victim/Caller: Evelyn Patel, resident of 1457 Cedarbrook Drive.
Suspect: Julian Sanchez, ex-romantic partner of Ms. Patel. Ms. Patel described Sanchez as approximately 32 years old, approximately 5'10" tall, medium build, brown hair, brown eyes, with a tattoo of a snake on his left arm and a scar above his right eyebrow. Ms. Patel provided a phone number for Sanchez, which she stated is the same number from which the unwanted calls and texts have originated. She stated she does not know his current address, as they had previously lived together but she does not know where he is living now.
Witnesses: Rachel Lee and Michael Kim, both friends of Ms. Patel, who independently observed Sanchez lurking around Ms. Patel's apartment complex approximately two to three days prior to this report. Ms. Patel stated she would provide their contact information as a follow-up.

RELATIONSHIP HISTORY

Ms. Patel stated she and Julian Sanchez were in a romantic relationship for approximately two years. She described the relationship as tumultuous, involving frequent arguments, verbal intimidation by Sanchez, and one incident of physical violence. Ms. Patel stated that approximately six months ago, during an argument, Sanchez became angry and pushed her, causing her to fall. She described that incident as "really scary." After the physical incident, she stated she attempted to stay away from Sanchez, but he would not leave her alone. She stated the relationship ended a few months prior to this report, and that the unwanted contact began almost immediately after the breakup.

STALKING BEHAVIOR — COURSE OF CONDUCT

Ms. Patel reported an ongoing and escalating pattern of unwanted contact by Sanchez since the end of their relationship a few months ago. She described the following repeated behaviors:

Calls and texts: Sanchez has been calling Ms. Patel every day, sometimes multiple times per day. He has sent a large volume of text messages. Ms. Patel stated she has saved the text messages and call records on her phone, including messages she described as threatening.

Showing up in person: Sanchez has appeared at Ms. Patel's apartment at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive approximately every other day. He has appeared at her downtown workplace (where she works as an administrative assistant) at least five or six times. He has also appeared multiple times at her favorite coffee shop. On one occasion, Sanchez showed up at Ms. Patel's sister's house, which Ms. Patel described as frightening to her sister as well. Ms. Patel stated she has told Sanchez to leave her alone and to stop contacting her, but the conduct has continued.

Surveillance and monitoring: Sanchez sent messages to Ms. Patel stating that he was watching her and that he knew where she was at any given time. For example, Ms. Patel stated that she would be at a store or at a friend's house, and Sanchez would send a message such as "I know you're there." Ms. Patel stated this conduct made her feel like he was always watching her.

Unwanted gifts: Sanchez has repeatedly sent flowers and gifts to Ms. Patel, despite being told to stop. Ms. Patel stated she has maintained a log of the dates and times the flowers and gifts were sent.

Third-party contact: Sanchez attempted to contact Ms. Patel through mutual friends. Ms. Patel stated she told those friends not to discuss her with Sanchez.

Social media contact: Sanchez sent Ms. Patel messages through Facebook and Instagram. Ms. Patel has blocked Sanchez on both platforms. Prior to blocking him, she took screenshots of the messages, which she stated are saved on her phone.

Today's incident: At approximately 1330 hours, Ms. Patel returned home from work and found a bouquet of flowers left outside the door of her apartment at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive. A handwritten note was attached. Ms. Patel stated she did not order the flowers and immediately recognized they were from Sanchez. She stated she did not touch the note at first. The note was handwritten in what Ms. Patel described as messy handwriting that appeared hurried. The note read: "I'll never let you go." Ms. Patel stated that seeing those words made her feel "really scared and trapped." The bouquet and note remained at Ms. Patel's apartment at the time of this report, untouched, and are available as physical evidence.

THREATS AND EXACT STATEMENTS

Ms. Patel reported the following specific statements made by Sanchez, to the best of her recollection:

Written note found on the bouquet today: "I'll never let you go."

Text messages from Sanchez included: "I'll never let you go" and "You'll never be able to get away from me."

In text messages, Sanchez stated he was watching Ms. Patel and that he knew where she was.

In verbal and phone communications on multiple occasions, Sanchez stated that if Ms. Patel did not come back to him, "he'll make sure I regret it." He also stated "he'll never let me go, and that I'll always be his."

Ms. Patel stated she had recorded one of Sanchez's voicemails, in which he made statements she described as "really scary things." That recording is saved on her phone.

Ms. Patel reported telling Sanchez on multiple occasions to leave her alone and to stop contacting her. She stated she tried to be firm, but the behavior continued.

VICTIM'S EMOTIONAL STATE AND IMPACT ON DAILY LIFE

Ms. Patel stated she has been significantly affected by Sanchez's conduct. She reported that she avoids going out as much as possible, is constantly looking over her shoulder, and has changed her routine in an attempt to be unpredictable. She stated this is exhausting. She reported difficulty sleeping because she is always worried Sanchez will show up at her apartment. She described feeling anxious and scared "all the time." Ms. Patel stated she is most afraid that Sanchez will hurt her or corner her somewhere she cannot get away. She stated she felt like she was "walking on eggshells all the time, waiting for him to do something else." At the time of this interview, she stated she did not feel safe returning to her apartment.

WITNESSES

Rachel Lee and Michael Kim, friends of Ms. Patel, both independently observed Sanchez lurking around Ms. Patel's apartment complex approximately two to three days prior to this report. Ms. Patel relayed that both witnesses told her they saw Sanchez hanging around the complex and looking around, which they described as suspicious and which frightened them. Contact information for Rachel Lee and Michael Kim was noted as a follow-up item, to be obtained from Ms. Patel.

EVIDENCE

Physical evidence: A bouquet of flowers with a handwritten threatening note reading "I'll never let you go" was located outside Ms. Patel's apartment door at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive, where it remained untouched at the time of this report. The bouquet and note are to be photographed and preserved as evidence.

Electronic evidence on Ms. Patel's phone: Saved text messages from Sanchez, including messages containing threats; a call log documenting the frequency of Sanchez's calls; a recorded voicemail from Sanchez containing statements Ms. Patel described as threatening; and screenshots of messages Sanchez sent to Ms. Patel via Facebook and Instagram prior to Ms. Patel blocking him on both platforms.

Victim's personal log: Ms. Patel stated she has maintained a personal log documenting the dates and times Sanchez appeared at her apartment and workplace, and the dates and times flowers and gifts were delivered.

Security camera footage: The apartment building at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive has a building security camera system that covers the lobby and parking lot. The camera is motion-activated. Ms. Patel stated she believes the footage is stored for a few weeks. The building manager or landlord will need to be contacted as soon as possible to preserve any footage that may show Sanchez's appearances at the building. This was documented as a priority follow-up action.

PROTECTION ORDER / FIREARMS / WARRANTS

Ms. Patel stated there is no protection order currently in place against Sanchez. She stated she has thought about obtaining one but has not done so yet. She stated she does not know of any prior protection orders that have been issued against Sanchez. I advised Ms. Patel about the process for seeking a protection order.

Ms. Patel stated she does not believe Sanchez owns firearms and has never seen him with a weapon. She stated they never discussed guns. She stated Sanchez's conduct has involved verbal threats and physical presence rather than weapons.

Ms. Patel stated she does not know of any prior arrests or active warrants for Sanchez. She stated she has never known him to have had prior police contact.

INJURIES / MEDICAL

Ms. Patel reported no injuries from today's incident. She described the prior physical incident, which occurred approximately six months ago during the relationship, in which Sanchez pushed her during an argument, causing her to fall and sustain minor bruising. Ms. Patel stated the bruising was not serious and she did not seek medical attention at that time. She stated she does not currently require medical attention. She described herself as physically safe at the time of this interview.

SAFETY PLANNING AND RESOURCES

Ms. Patel stated she does not feel safe returning to her apartment and planned to stay at a friend's house. I provided Ms. Patel with information about protection orders and local victim advocacy services. I discussed with Ms. Patel the use of a stalking log to document any future incidents, including date, time, location, description of conduct, and any witnesses. I documented the following as follow-up actions: (1) contact building management at 1457 Cedarbrook Drive to preserve security camera footage; (2) photograph and collect the bouquet and note from Ms. Patel's apartment; (3) obtain contact information for witnesses Rachel Lee and Michael Kim from Ms. Patel; (4) review electronic evidence on Ms. Patel's phone including text messages, call log, voicemail recording, and social media screenshots; (5) conduct a check on Julian Sanchez for warrants and criminal history.

OFFICER ACTIONS

I responded to the police station, where Ms. Patel had presented after calling for help. I conducted a thorough interview of Ms. Patel regarding the reported stalking conduct. I documented the physical and electronic evidence described by Ms. Patel. I advised Ms. Patel regarding protection orders, local victim services, and stalking log documentation. I identified priority follow-up items including evidence preservation, witness follow-up, and a records check on the suspect. No arrest was made at this time.
